step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the previous assessment. Option B states "Image is visual and erect," which is indeed false because the image seen through an indirect ophthalmoscope is actually inverted relative to its position on the retina. This inversion occurs due to the use of a concave mirror or condensing lens, making the image appear flipped both vertically and horizontally upon examination. Therefore, my answer remains clear: **Option B is correct**, indicating why it is false. .
